title: Re-audit skills on mutation, not only on initial import
description: Mira's HARD RULE #4 requires `soul_manager.audit_skill()` for 'newly generated or imported' skills. But Mira also has a 'Prompt Self-Mutation' learned skill that evolves existing skill files. A skill that passed audit at import time can be mutated into an unsafe form and re-saved without ever being re-audited. The fix: ensure `soul_manager` calls `audit_skill()` on every skill write/update path, not just the initial import gate.
rationale: The reading note's core point: contracts (one-time gates) fail when the adversary can operate after the gate. Technical locks are only more reliable than contracts if they're enforced continuously, not just at entry. Mira's audit is currently a gate at import time — a contract, not a technical lock. Self-mutation (a capability Mira already has) is exactly the 'adversary operating after the gate.' Re-auditing on every write closes this gap and makes the audit an actual technical lock rather than a one-time promise.
diff_description: In `soul_manager.py`, find the function(s) responsible for saving or updating skill files (likely `save_skill()`, `update_skill()`, or wherever skill file writes happen). Add a call to `audit_skill()` on the new content before the write is committed. If the audit fails, block the write and log it the same way initial import failures are handled. This mirrors what already exists for imports — it just extends the invariant to cover the mutation path.
